## What does Paramanu mean? **Paramanu** (परमाणु) is a Sanskrit term meaning **atom** — the smallest, indivisible particle of matter. In the ancient Indian Vaisheshika school of philosophy, paramanu represented the fundamental building blocks from which all physical reality is composed. This design system carries the same idea: **the smallest, composable building blocks for crafting web interfaces**. Each token, style, and component is a paramanu — minimal, purposeful, and designed to combine into something greater.

## Why Paramanu? Built for teams that care about performance, accessibility, and developer experience.
🎨

CSS-First, Zero Runtime

Built entirely on CSS custom properties and @layer cascade ordering. No JavaScript needed for styling — your buttons, inputs, and layouts work without a framework.

🧩

Three Consumption Modes

Drop in via CDN with human-readable classes, use as CSS Modules for scoped styles, or import React components with full type safety.

♥

Accessible by Default

WCAG 2.2 AA compliant. Focus-visible indicators, semantic HTML, ARIA attributes, and automated axe-core testing baked in.

✨

Modern CSS

CSS nesting, light-dark() for automatic dark mode, @layer for cascade control, container queries, and @scope.

🎭

5 Built-in Themes

Material, Ant Design, Bootstrap, Dark Modern, and Light Modern. Swap a class name to re-skin your entire app instantly.

📦

Tree-Shakeable

Import only what you use. Each component group is a separate package — no monolithic bundles, no unused CSS.

## Choose Your Framework Use Paramanu with vanilla JavaScript or React — same design tokens, same CSS, same components.

JavaScript / CSS

Framework-agnostic class builders and CSS. Works with any templating engine, SSR framework, or plain HTML.

  • BEM class builder functions
  • CSS Modules support
  • Zero framework dependencies
  • Works with Astro, Svelte, Vue, htmx

## Design Principles

Minimal by Design

Every component does one thing well. No bloated bundles, no hidden dependencies, no surprise side effects.

Composable

Tokens feed into components, components combine into patterns. Override any layer without fighting specificity wars.

Progressive Enhancement

Starts with semantic HTML and CSS. Layer on JavaScript interactivity only when needed. Works without JS, shines with it.

Themeable

Swap design tokens at the root level to re-skin everything. Built-in light-dark() support means dark mode with one CSS property.

## Package Manager

Install the group packages you need. Each component group is a separate package:

```bash
# Design tokens (always needed)
pnpm add @paramanu/tokens

# Btns (JS class builders + CSS)
pnpm add @paramanu/buttons-js

# Btns (React wrappers, optional)
pnpm add @paramanu/buttons-react
```

Available groups: `primitives`, `typography`, `buttons`, `forms`, `navigation`, `data-display`, `feedback`, `overlays`, `disclosure`, `utilities`.

Each group follows the pattern `@paramanu/<group>-js` and `@paramanu/<group>-react`.

## Accessibility

- Uses semantic `<button>` element by default
- Focus-visible ring for keyboard navigation (WCAG 2.2 AA compliant)
- Disabled buttons set both `aria-disabled="true"` and the `disabled` attribute
- Loading buttons set `aria-busy="true"` and are automatically disabled
- Supports `aria-label` for icon-only usage

## Best Practices

- **Use the right variant** for the action's importance: `primary` for the main action, `secondary` for alternatives, `danger` for destructive actions, `ghost`/`outline` for low-emphasis actions, and `link` for inline text actions.
- **One primary button per section** to avoid competing calls to action.
- **Use clear, concise labels** with action verbs like "Save", "Delete", "Submit".
- **Indicate loading states** for async actions to prevent double-clicks.
- **Always include a `type` attribute** in forms.

## Do's and Don'ts

| Do                                                  | Don't                                            |
| --------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| Use `variant="danger"` for destructive actions      | Style primary buttons red with custom CSS        |
| Disable buttons when action is unavailable          | Hide buttons and confuse users                   |
| Use `fullWidth` for mobile or single-action layouts | Use `fullWidth` for every button                 |
| Provide `aria-label` when using icon-only buttons   | Leave icon-only buttons without accessible names |

## CSS Classes

| Class                      | Purpose           |
| -------------------------- | ----------------- |
| `pm-box`                   | Base box styles   |
| `pm-box--d-{value}`        | Display modifier  |
| `pm-box--p-{scale}`        | Padding all sides |
| `pm-box--px-{scale}`       | Padding inline    |
| `pm-box--py-{scale}`       | Padding block     |
| `pm-box--m-{scale}`        | Margin all sides  |
| `pm-box--mx-{scale}`       | Margin inline     |
| `pm-box--my-{scale}`       | Margin block      |
| `pm-box--overflow-{value}` | Overflow modifier |
| `pm-box--pos-{value}`      | Position modifier |

## Accessibility

Box renders a `<div>` by default which has no semantic meaning. Use the `as` prop to render a semantically appropriate element when needed (e.g., `section`, `article`).

## Best Practices

- Use Box as a generic wrapper when no more specific primitive (Flex, Stack, Grid) fits.

Locale-aware number formatting using the Intl.NumberFormat API.

## API

| Prop       | Type                                      | Default      | Description            |
| ---------- | ----------------------------------------- | ------------ | ---------------------- |
| `value`    | `number`                                  | -            | The number to format   |
| `locale`   | `string`                                  | `"en-US"`    | BCP 47 language tag    |
| `style`    | `"decimal" \| "currency" \| "percent"`    | `"decimal"`  | Formatting style       |
| `currency` | `string`                                  | -            | ISO 4217 currency code |
| `notation` | `"standard" \| "compact" \| "scientific"` | `"standard"` | Notation style         |
| `as`       | `ElementType`                             | `"span"`     | HTML element to render |

- Always specify a `currency` when using `style="currency"`
- Use `notation="compact"` for large numbers in space-constrained UIs
- The locale defaults to `"en-US"` but inherits from LocaleProvider context
- Renders as a `<span>` by default; use the `as` prop to change the element

Manages mount/unmount animation lifecycle for enter and exit transitions.

## API

| Prop        | Type         | Default | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------------ | ------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `present`   | `boolean`    | -       | Whether the element should be visible |
| `duration`  | `number`     | `200`   | Transition duration in milliseconds   |
| `onEntered` | `() => void` | -       | Callback when enter completes         |
| `onExited`  | `() => void` | -       | Callback when exit completes          |

- Match the `duration` prop to your CSS transition/animation duration
- Use `usePresence` hook for custom animation logic
- States flow: `exited` -> `entering` -> `entered` -> `exiting` -> `exited`
- Supports render props for state-based class application

A CSS-driven visibility toggle that preserves DOM state.

## API

| Prop      | Type               | Default  | Description          |
| --------- | ------------------ | -------- | -------------------- |
| `display` | `"show" \| "hide"` | `"show"` | The visibility state |

- Use when you need to preserve internal state while hiding content
- Use conditional rendering (`{show && <Child />}`) when you want to fully unmount
- `pm-show` uses `display: revert` to preserve the element's original display type
- `pm-hide` uses `display: none`

Manages light/dark/system color scheme with localStorage persistence.

## API

| Prop          | Type                            | Default      | Description                      |
| ------------- | ------------------------------- | ------------ | -------------------------------- |
| `defaultMode` | `"light" \| "dark" \| "system"` | `"system"`   | Initial color scheme             |
| `storageKey`  | `string`                        | `"pm-theme"` | localStorage key for persistence |

- Wrap your app root with `Theme` for consistent theming
- Use `"system"` as default to respect user OS preference
- The `useTheme` hook provides read/write access to the current mode
- Theme preference persists across page reloads via `localStorage`
